input:
N0:16173
N P: BAGHDAD MOHAMED ISLEM
Born on 09/24/2022
Address: HASSI BOUNIF ORAN
Tel No: 0698549428 | Date 01/12/2025 | Date Entry: 18-11-2025
Release Date: 02/12/2025
N0:16173
N P: BAGHDAD MOHAMED ISLEM
Born on 09/24/2022
Address: HASSI BOUNIF ORAN
Tel No: 0698549428 | Resident: DR.TAMAZIRT | Assistant: Dr OUSSALAH
REHOSPITALIZATION FORM
ProfessorHead of Department
M.O. HABIB
Family history:
Father: 46 years old, asthmatic
Mother: 29 years old, in good health
Siblings: The last of a sibling of 02. Sister suffering from cystic fibrosis followed at our level:
Baghdad Malek Tasnim born in 2020 File number: 15561
Personal history:
Physiological:
Well-monitored pregnancy
Full-term birth, BW: 3kg700
Meconium emitted < 24hrs
Mixed breastfeeding since birth
Dietary diversification at the age of 6 months
Up to date vaccinations
Good psychomotor development
Pathological:
Cystic fibrosis with dual digestive and respiratory expression since the age of 2 months. History of the disease
Primary diagnosis: Cystic fibrosis
Age of diagnosis: 02 months
Diagnostic elements:
Anamnestics:
Consanguinity: 3rd degree
Similar case in the family: his sister
Clinic:
Recurrent hacking cough since the age of 2 and a half months with bronchial congestion
Chronic oily diarrhea since the age of 2 months
Paraclinic:
02 sweat test coming back positive
Fecal elastase: 0.3µg/g
Processing in progress:
- For respiratory purposes:
Respiratory physiotherapy at home twice daily
- For nutritional purposes:
Opotherapy replacement: Creon 12000 IU 6 capsules per day or 4100 IU/day
Alvityl 1 tsp/d
Vit K 10 mg/15 days
Vit D 01 ampoule/3 months
Vit E 01cp/d
Therapeutic compliance: Good
Events since the last visit:
Hospitalized for exacerbation and clarification on 07/31/2025
Consultation: yes
Respiratory: intermittent productive cough, whitish sputum, eupneic
On the digestive level: number of stools 1-2 stools/day, transit preserved, appetite preserved. Nutritionally: good nutritional status. On the ENT level: RAS. Activity: yes. Schooling: no. Reason for hospitalization: Exacerbation of his illness
History of illness:
Goes back a few days before his admission, marked by the onset of a flu syndrome with a change in the color and consistency of the sputum, hence his admission. CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION:
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 17kg 500 [+1.82DS]
Height: 100cm [+0.72DS] Normal nutritional status
BMI: 17.5 (+1.46DS) between P75-P90
General condition preserved, conscious cooperating, T: 37.6°C. Normocolored integuments and conjunctivae. ENT examination:
Nasal obstruction. Pharyngitis. No cervical lymphadenopathy. Cardiovascular examination:
HR: 114b/m, B1 B2 audible. No hiss or added noises. Respiratory symptoms:
FR:44c/m SaO2:98% under ambient air
AP: clear. Intermittent cough, thick whitish sputum
no respiratory distress, no clubbing. Digestive/nutritional symptoms:
Abdomen soft on palpation. No collateral circulation, no organomegaly
Stools of normal consistency 1 to 2 stools/day (oily sometimes)
No GERD or rectal prolapse
Neurological examination:
Conscious patient 15/15 on the Glasgow score. No motor or sensory problems, good DPM
Examination of the skin and appendages:
Standard colored integuments
No damage to the scalp or skin appendages. Osteoarticular examination:
No limitation of movements
Support:
Multiple daily physiotherapy
Biantibiotherapy according to the antibiogram
Blood tests and ECBC
Interview with dietitian
Interview with psychologist
Interview with respiratory physiotherapy
Monitoring
Complete the development and impact of your illness
Evolution:
On day 02:
Complete assessment has been launched:
Hematology:
Hb: 13.5g/dl Leukocytes: 9500/mm³. Platelets: 413,000/mm³.
CRP: negative Procalcitonin: negative
Biochemistry:
HbA1c: 5.81%
Na+: 132 mmol/L Cl⁻: 103.6 mmol/LK+: 3.83 mmol/L
ASAT/ALAT: 36.3/23.6 IU/L
Urea/creat: 0.19/4.9
cholesterol: 1.52 g/Ltriglycerides: 0.47 g/L
Albumin: 39.7g/l
Ferritinemia: 4.83 ng/ml Vitamin D: 1
Calcemia: 98 mg/l Phosphoria: 50 mg/l
CAT: Vitamin D Supplementation
On day 03:
Dietary maintenance: Good nutritional status
Maintenance and updating of physiotherapy with the mother: Effective and correct physiotherapy technique
ECBC Results:
Sputum culture (11/18/2025): bacterial: Ralstonia Pickettii sensitive to fortum. So we continued the same antibiotic therapy. Yeasts and mushrooms: Negative. During her hospitalization which lasted 15 days, she received 14 days of Fortum and 10 days of Amikacin with a good clinical evolution, reduction in the consistency of the sputum; outgoing on 02-12-2025 with control appointment at the specialized consultation with influenza vaccine. Development and impact of his illness:
On the respiratory level:
Clinically:
Clinically:
No chest deformity
No digital clubbing
Paraclinic:
Chest x-ray: unremarkable
Thoracic CT (08/20/2025): chest distension
Gait test: normal. Aspergillus serology (08-21-2025): Negative
Arterial blood gas analysis requested. Microbiology:
Sputum culture (18/112025): bacterial: RASTONIA PICKETTI sensitive to fortum
Yeasts and mushrooms: negative. On the cardiac level:
Clinically: B1 B2 audible. No breath or added noise
Cardiac ultrasound (12-2024): LV EF: 60%, NO dilation or hypertrophy of the cavities, normal right pressure 15 mmHg (absence of PAH)
On the ENT level:
Nasal obstruction, no rhinorrhea. No smell problem. On the digestive level:
Clinic:
Abdomen soft on palpation. No collateral circulation, no organomegaly
Stools of normal consistency 1 to 2 stools/day
Paraclinic:
Abdomino-pelvic ultrasound (09/01/2025): Unremarkable
Digestive and pancreatic assessment: Fecal elastase: 0.3 µg/g
Nutritionally:
Clinic:
No failure to thrive
Good nutritional status
Paraclinic:
Vitamin K: (TP = 81.6%)
Vitamin D: 24.6 ng/ml
Cholesterol: 1.52 correct
Triglycerides: 0.47g/l correct
on a psychological level:
Supportive psychotherapy. CONCLUSION:
This is the child BAGHDAD MOHAMED RAYANE aged 03 years old from MOSATGANEM and residing in ORAN, from a 3rd degree consanguineous couple followed in our department since the age of 2 months for Cystic fibrosis with double respiratory and digestive expression on the phenotypic level admitted to our department on 11/18/2025 for development and management of an exacerbation of his illness. Release order:
Prescription for flu vaccine
Respiratory physiotherapy at home twice daily
Opotherapy replacement therapy: Creon 12000 IU 08 capsules per day, i.e.: 4800 IU/day
Alvityl 1 tsp/d
Vit K 10 mg/15 days
Bulb life/2 months
Vit E 1cp/d
Please recover:
Arterial blood gas results. Resident: Dr. Tamazirt Assistant: Dr. Oussalah. ProfessorHead of Department
M.O. HABIB
